title: Inheritance Patterns and ES6 Classes

learningObjectives.text: Explain constructor-function inheritance using a new example and justify the result.
Explain `object.create` inheritance using a new example and justify the result.
Apply es6 class syntax and comparison using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns inheritance patterns and es6 classes from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Object-Oriented JavaScript** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Constructor-function inheritance** → **`Object.create` inheritance** → **ES6 class syntax and comparison**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Constructor-function inheritance
sections.body: Constructor inheritance initializes parent-owned fields and links child and parent prototypes for shared methods.

sections.title: `Object.create` inheritance
sections.body: Direct delegation builds one prototype object from another and creates instances that follow that chain.

sections.title: ES6 class syntax and comparison
sections.body: Class syntax provides constructors, methods, static methods, extends, and super while still using prototype delegation underneath.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: `extends` links prototypes and `super` initializes parent-owned state.

commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.
summary: Inheritance Patterns and ES6 Classes connects Constructor-function inheritance, `Object.create` inheritance, ES6 class syntax and comparison.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
